reviews.review_msg: Sasha Colby is one of the best professors I have ever been with. Passionate, very knowledgeable, fair and a terrific facilitator. I've taken two classes with her and would take another given the chance.
Sasha is one of the best professors I have had. A 10 out of 10 in all areas.  Warm, friendly, great presenter and excellent facilitator.

Sasha is amazing. She is funny, approachable, respectful of everyone's opinions, and a fair marker, all around the ideal prof. The exams and assignments were very fair. She made WL a very enjoyable elective.

Sasha is seriously amazing! She's friendly, kind, encouraging, and funny. She really listens to what everyone has to say, and doesn't discredit anyone. She's passionate about the subject, and very clear on assignment expectations. She's also a very fair marker! Love her.

SO incredibly helpful - and perfectly clear in her expectations, critiques, etc. I have never had a prof care so much about my personal improvement! I became a MUCH stronger writer because of Sasha Colby!!

Super professor. So enthusiastic, so interesting, pretty funny too. She is my fav prof so far at SFU. She speaks very eloquently and this makes her very understandable because she is able to say precisely what students need to hear to understand material. She will teach you many very useful skills. I quickly signed up for another of her classes!

A bit of an airhead, but in a good way. Very artsy and creative, recognizes ingenuity in projects. A bit too politically vocal for an English prof (leftist of some sort) inserting her opinions on topical affairs into relation to  the course in a didactic manner.

sasha's the best prof i've had. going into the class i really didn't like her sense of humour, but after getting together and asking her tons of ques one on one, she's extremely helpful and super nice! very easy course if you know what the prof and t.a are looking for in the essays. sooo easy to get good marks if you just read the books assigned!!

Interesting and entertaining professor. One must keep up with the readings to fully appreciate the course. It is much like an english course, quite writing intensive, so beware! She seems to be genuinely interested in helping her students improve as writers. Approachable within tutorial and office hours. Overall, good professor for an okay course.

Sasha is great, her feedback on your work is REALLY helpful and she genuinely cares about her students. Creative and fun, but heck, if lectures get a little long, you can always comment on her outfits ;)
